content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
AI Infrastructure Jobs in Malaysia — Skills Gap Heatmap
Compares market demand against skills demonstrated by the Corvia project.
"""

import matplotlib
matplotlib.use('Agg')
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.patches as mpatches
import numpy as np

# ── Skill categories and individual skills ──────────────────────────────
categories = [
    "Programming",
    "ML Frameworks",
    "Cloud Platforms",
    "Containers & Orch.",
    "Data & Databases",
    "MLOps Tools",
    "CI/CD & IaC",
    "Monitoring",
    "AI/LLM & NLP",
    "Architecture",
    "API & Protocols",
    "Certifications",
]

skills = [
    # Programming
    ("Python", "Programming"),
    ("Rust", "Programming"),
    ("Java", "Programming"),
    ("SQL", "Programming"),
    ("Shell/Bash", "Programming"),
    # ML Frameworks
    ("TensorFlow / Keras", "ML Frameworks"),
    ("PyTorch", "ML Frameworks"),
    ("Scikit-learn", "ML Frameworks"),
    ("Hugging Face", "ML Frameworks"),
    # Cloud Platforms
    ("AWS (SageMaker, EC2, S3)", "Cloud Platforms"),
    ("Azure (Azure ML)", "Cloud Platforms"),
    ("GCP (Vertex AI)", "Cloud Platforms"),
    # Containers & Orchestration
    ("Docker", "Containers & Orch."),
    ("Kubernetes", "Containers & Orch."),
    ("Helm / Compose", "Containers & Orch."),
    # Data & Databases
    ("SQL / Relational DBs", "Data & Databases"),
    ("NoSQL (MongoDB, etc.)", "Data & Databases"),
    ("Vector DBs (Pinecone, etc.)", "Data & Databases"),
    ("Apache Spark", "Data & Databases"),
    ("Apache Kafka", "Data & Databases"),
    # MLOps Tools
    ("MLflow", "MLOps Tools"),
    ("Kubeflow", "MLOps Tools"),
    ("DVC / Data Versioning", "MLOps Tools"),
    ("Model Serving (TorchServe)", "MLOps Tools"),
    ("Weights & Biases", "MLOps Tools"),
    # CI/CD & IaC
    ("Git (advanced)", "CI/CD & IaC"),
    ("CI/CD Pipelines", "CI/CD & IaC"),
    ("Terraform / Ansible", "CI/CD & IaC"),
    ("CloudFormation", "CI/CD & IaC"),
    # Monitoring
    ("Prometheus / Grafana", "Monitoring"),
    ("ELK Stack", "Monitoring"),
    ("Model Drift Monitoring", "Monitoring"),
    # AI/LLM & NLP
    ("LLM Integration (Ollama, vLLM)", "AI/LLM & NLP"),
    ("Embeddings & Vector Search", "AI/LLM & NLP"),
    ("RAG Pipelines", "AI/LLM & NLP"),
    ("Prompt Engineering", "AI/LLM & NLP"),
    ("Fine-tuning / RLHF", "AI/LLM & NLP"),
    # Architecture
    ("Distributed Systems", "Architecture"),
    ("Concurrency / Async", "Architecture"),
    ("Multi-agent Coordination", "Architecture"),
    ("Crash Recovery / Idempotency", "Architecture"),
    ("RBAC / Access Control", "Architecture"),
    # API & Protocols
    ("REST API Design", "API & Protocols"),
    ("gRPC / Protobuf", "API & Protocols"),
    ("MCP (Model Context Protocol)", "API & Protocols"),
    ("WebSocket / Streaming", "API & Protocols"),
    # Certifications
    ("AWS ML Specialty", "Certifications"),
    ("Azure AI Engineer Assoc.", "Certifications"),
    ("TensorFlow Developer Cert.", "Certifications"),
    ("GCP Professional ML Eng.", "Certifications"),
]

skill_names = [s[0] for s in skills]
skill_cats = [s[1] for s in skills]
n = len(skills)

# ── Job role columns ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
roles = [
    "AI Engineer",
    "ML Engineer",
    "MLOps\nEngineer",
    "AI Infra\nEngineer",
    "AI Platform\nEngineer",
]
n_roles = len(roles)

# ── Market demand matrix (0=Not required, 1=Nice-to-have, 2=Important, 3=Critical) ──
# Rows = skills, Cols = roles
demand = np.array([
    # AiEng  MLEng  MLOps  AIInfra AIPlatf
    [  3,     3,     3,     2,      3  ],  # Python
    [  0,     0,     0,     1,      0  ],  # Rust
    [  1,     1,     1,     2,      1  ],  # Java
    [  2,     2,     2,     2,      2  ],  # SQL
    [  1,     1,     2,     3,      2  ],  # Shell/Bash
    [  3,     3,     2,     1,      2  ],  # TensorFlow/Keras
    [  3,     3,     2,     1,      2  ],  # PyTorch
    [  2,     3,     1,     0,      1  ],  # Scikit-learn
    [  3,     2,     1,     0,      1  ],  # Hugging Face
    [  2,     2,     3,     3,      3  ],  # AWS
    [  2,     2,     3,     3,      3  ],  # Azure
    [  2,     2,     3,     3,      3  ],  # GCP
    [  2,     2,     3,     3,      3  ],  # Docker
    [  1,     2,     3,     3,      3  ],  # Kubernetes
    [  1,     1,     2,     2,      2  ],  # Helm/Compose
    [  2,     2,     2,     2,      2  ],  # SQL/Relational
    [  2,     2,     1,     2,      2  ],  # NoSQL
    [  2,     1,     1,     2,      2  ],  # Vector DBs
    [  1,     2,     2,     2,      1  ],  # Apache Spark
    [  1,     1,     2,     2,      1  ],  # Apache Kafka
    [  1,     2,     3,     2,      2  ],  # MLflow
    [  1,     1,     3,     2,      2  ],  # Kubeflow
    [  1,     2,     2,     1,      1  ],  # DVC
    [  2,     2,     3,     2,      3  ],  # Model Serving
    [  1,     2,     2,     1,      1  ],  # W&B
    [  2,     2,     3,     3,      2  ],  # Git advanced
    [  2,     2,     3,     3,      3  ],  # CI/CD
    [  1,     1,     2,     3,      3  ],  # Terraform/Ansible
    [  0,     0,     1,     2,      2  ],  # CloudFormation
    [  1,     1,     2,     3,      2  ],  # Prometheus/Grafana
    [  1,     1,     2,     2,      1  ],  # ELK Stack
    [  2,     2,     3,     1,      2  ],  # Model Drift Monitor
    [  3,     2,     1,     2,      2  ],  # LLM Integration
    [  3,     2,     1,     2,      2  ],  # Embeddings & Vector Search
    [  3,     2,     1,     1,      2  ],  # RAG Pipelines
    [  3,     1,     1,     1,      1  ],  # Prompt Engineering
    [  3,     3,     1,     1,      1  ],  # Fine-tuning/RLHF
    [  2,     2,     2,     3,      3  ],  # Distributed Systems
    [  2,     2,     2,     3,      2  ],  # Concurrency/Async
    [  2,     1,     1,     2,      1  ],  # Multi-agent Coordination
    [  1,     1,     2,     3,      2  ],  # Crash Recovery
    [  1,     1,     2,     2,      2  ],  # RBAC
    [  3,     2,     2,     2,      3  ],  # REST API
    [  1,     1,     1,     2,      2  ],  # gRPC
    [  2,     1,     0,     1,      1  ],  # MCP
    [  2,     1,     1,     2,      2  ],  # WebSocket/Streaming
    [  1,     1,     2,     2,      2  ],  # AWS ML Cert
    [  1,     1,     2,     2,      2  ],  # Azure AI Cert
    [  2,     2,     1,     0,      1  ],  # TF Dev Cert
    [  1,     1,     2,     2,      2  ],  # GCP ML Cert
])

# ── Your proficiency from Corvia (0=None, 1=Basic, 2=Moderate, 3=Strong) ──
corvia_level = np.array([
    1,  # Python (only md2pdf.py script)
    3,  # Rust (entire project, 8477 LOC)
    0,  # Java
    2,  # SQL (SurrealDB queries)
    2,  # Shell/Bash (provisioning scripts)
    0,  # TensorFlow/Keras
    0,  # PyTorch
    0,  # Scikit-learn
    0,  # Hugging Face
    0,  # AWS
    0,  # Azure
    0,  # GCP
    2,  # Docker (Bollard SDK, compose, devcontainers)
    0,  # Kubernetes
    2,  # Helm/Compose (docker-compose.yml)
    2,  # SQL/Relational (Redb, SurrealDB)
    2,  # NoSQL (SurrealDB schemaless, Redb KV)
    3,  # Vector DBs (HNSW, SurrealDB vector indexes)
    0,  # Apache Spark
    0,  # Apache Kafka
    0,  # MLflow
    0,  # Kubeflow
    2,  # DVC (git-tracked JSON knowledge files)
    2,  # Model Serving (Ollama + vLLM management)
    0,  # W&B
    3,  # Git advanced (git2, branching, merge strategies)
    1,  # CI/CD (introspect --ci, basic)
    0,  # Terraform/Ansible
    0,  # CloudFormation
    0,  # Prometheus/Grafana
    0,  # ELK Stack
    0,  # Model Drift Monitoring
    3,  # LLM Integration (Ollama chat + embeddings, vLLM)
    3,  # Embeddings & Vector Search (full pipeline)
    3,  # RAG Pipelines (context builder, semantic search)
    2,  # Prompt Engineering (merge prompts)
    0,  # Fine-tuning/RLHF
    3,  # Distributed Systems (agent coordination, merge queue)
    3,  # Concurrency/Async (Tokio, async/await)
    3,  # Multi-agent Coordination (entire M2)
    3,  # Crash Recovery (idempotent commit pipeline)
    3,  # RBAC (visibility modes, scope-based access)
    3,  # REST API (Axum framework)
    0,  # gRPC
    3,  # MCP (JSON-RPC 2.0 implementation)
    1,  # WebSocket (SurrealDB client)
    0,  # AWS ML Cert
    0,  # Azure AI Cert
    0,  # TF Dev Cert
    0,  # GCP ML Cert
])

# ── Compute weighted gap ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Average demand across roles, then gap = avg_demand - your_level
avg_demand = demand.mean(axis=1)
gap = np.clip(avg_demand - corvia_level, -3, 3)

# ── Build the figure ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
fig = plt.figure(figsize=(22, 28), facecolor='#0d1117')

# Grid: main heatmap left, summary right
gs = fig.add_gridspec(1, 3, width_ratios=[5, 0.8, 1.8], wspace=0.05)
ax_heat = fig.add_subplot(gs[0, 0])
ax_you = fig.add_subplot(gs[0, 1])
ax_gap = fig.add_subplot(gs[0, 2])

# ── Color maps ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
from matplotlib.colors import LinearSegmentedColormap

demand_cmap = LinearSegmentedColormap.from_list('demand', [
    '#161b22',   # 0 - not required (dark)
    '#1a3a2a',   # 1 - nice to have (dim green)
    '#2ea043',   # 2 - important (green)
    '#56d364',   # 3 - critical (bright green)
])

you_cmap = LinearSegmentedColormap.from_list('you', [
    '#3d1a1a',   # 0 - none (dark red)
    '#6e4b1a',   # 1 - basic (amber)
    '#2a6099',   # 2 - moderate (blue)
    '#1f6feb',   # 3 - strong (bright blue)
])

gap_cmap = LinearSegmentedColormap.from_list('gap', [
    '#1f6feb',   # -3 surplus (blue)
    '#238636',   # 0 no gap (green)
    '#da3633',   # +3 big gap (red)
], N=256)

# ── Main demand heatmap ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
im1 = ax_heat.imshow(demand, aspect='auto', cmap=demand_cmap, vmin=0, vmax=3,
                      interpolation='nearest')

ax_heat.set_xticks(range(n_roles))
ax_heat.set_xticklabels(roles, fontsize=11, color='white', fontweight='bold')
ax_heat.xaxis.set_ticks_position('top')
ax_heat.xaxis.set_label_position('top')

ax_heat.set_yticks(range(n))
ax_heat.set_yticklabels(skill_names, fontsize=10, color='white')
ax_heat.tick_params(axis='both', length=0)

# Add demand value annotations
demand_labels = ['—', '○', '●', '★']
for i in range(n):
    for j in range(n_roles):
        val = demand[i, j]
        ax_heat.text(j, i, demand_labels[val], ha='center', va='center',
                     fontsize=11, color='white' if val >= 2 else '#8b949e',
                     fontweight='bold')

# ── Category separators and labels ───────────────────────────────────────
cat_boundaries = []
current_cat = skill_cats[0]
start = 0
for i, cat in enumerate(skill_cats):
    if cat != current_cat:
        cat_boundaries.append((start, i - 1, current_cat))
        start = i
        current_cat = cat
cat_boundaries.append((start, n - 1, current_cat))

for start_idx, end_idx, cat_name in cat_boundaries:
    if start_idx > 0:
        for ax in [ax_heat, ax_you, ax_gap]:
            ax.axhline(y=start_idx - 0.5, color='#30363d', linewidth=1.5)

# ── Your level column ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
you_2d = corvia_level.reshape(-1, 1)
im2 = ax_you.imshow(you_2d, aspect='auto', cmap=you_cmap, vmin=0, vmax=3,
                     interpolation='nearest')

ax_you.set_xticks([0])
ax_you.set_xticklabels(['Corvia\nDemo'], fontsize=11, color='#58a6ff', fontweight='bold')
ax_you.xaxis.set_ticks_position('top')
ax_you.xaxis.set_label_position('top')
ax_you.set_yticks([])
ax_you.tick_params(axis='both', length=0)

you_labels = ['✗', '~', '✓', '★']
for i in range(n):
    val = int(corvia_level[i])
    color = '#da3633' if val == 0 else '#d29922' if val == 1 else '#58a6ff' if val == 2 else '#56d364'
    ax_you.text(0, i, you_labels[val], ha='center', va='center',
                fontsize=13, color=color, fontweight='bold')

# ── Gap column ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
gap_2d = gap.reshape(-1, 1)
im3 = ax_gap.imshow(gap_2d, aspect='auto', cmap=gap_cmap, vmin=-3, vmax=3,
                     interpolation='nearest')

ax_gap.set_xticks([0])
ax_gap.set_xticklabels(['Skill Gap'], fontsize=11, color='white', fontweight='bold')
ax_gap.xaxis.set_ticks_position('top')
ax_gap.xaxis.set_label_position('top')
ax_gap.set_yticks([])
ax_gap.tick_params(axis='both', length=0)

for i in range(n):
    g = gap[i]
    if g > 1.5:
        label = "URGENT"
        color = '#ff7b72'
    elif g > 0.5:
        label = "Gap"
        color = '#d29922'
    elif g > -0.5:
        label = "OK"
        color = '#8b949e'
    else:
        label = "STRONG"
        color = '#56d364'
    ax_gap.text(0, i, label, ha='center', va='center',
                fontsize=9, color=color, fontweight='bold')

# ── Styling ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
for ax in [ax_heat, ax_you, ax_gap]:
    ax.set_facecolor('#0d1117')
    for spine in ax.spines.values():
        spine.set_color('#30363d')
        spine.set_linewidth(0.5)

# ── Title ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
fig.suptitle('AI Infrastructure Jobs in Malaysia — Skills Gap Analysis',
             fontsize=20, color='white', fontweight='bold', y=0.98)
fig.text(0.5, 0.965,
         'Market demand vs. skills demonstrated by Corvia (organizational reasoning memory system)',
         fontsize=12, color='#8b949e', ha='center')

# ── Legends ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
legend_y = 0.015

# Demand legend
demand_legend = [
    mpatches.Patch(facecolor='#161b22', edgecolor='#30363d', label='— Not Required'),
    mpatches.Patch(facecolor='#1a3a2a', edgecolor='#30363d', label='○ Nice-to-have'),
    mpatches.Patch(facecolor='#2ea043', edgecolor='#30363d', label='● Important'),
    mpatches.Patch(facecolor='#56d364', edgecolor='#30363d', label='★ Critical'),
]
leg1 = fig.legend(handles=demand_legend, loc='lower left', bbox_to_anchor=(0.02, legend_y),
                  ncol=4, fontsize=10, facecolor='#161b22', edgecolor='#30363d',
                  labelcolor='white', title='Market Demand', title_fontsize=11)
leg1.get_title().set_color('#58a6ff')

# Your level legend
you_legend = [
    mpatches.Patch(facecolor='#3d1a1a', edgecolor='#30363d', label='✗ None'),
    mpatches.Patch(facecolor='#6e4b1a', edgecolor='#30363d', label='~ Basic'),
    mpatches.Patch(facecolor='#2a6099', edgecolor='#30363d', label='✓ Moderate'),
    mpatches.Patch(facecolor='#1f6feb', edgecolor='#30363d', label='★ Strong'),
]
leg2 = fig.legend(handles=you_legend, loc='lower right', bbox_to_anchor=(0.98, legend_y),
                  ncol=4, fontsize=10, facecolor='#161b22', edgecolor='#30363d',
                  labelcolor='white', title='Your Level (via Corvia)', title_fontsize=11)
leg2.get_title().set_color('#58a6ff')

# Gap legend (center)
gap_legend = [
    mpatches.Patch(facecolor='#1f6feb', edgecolor='#30363d', label='STRONG (surplus)'),
    mpatches.Patch(facecolor='#238636', edgecolor='#30363d', label='OK (matched)'),
    mpatches.Patch(facecolor='#d29922', edgecolor='#30363d', label='Gap (moderate)'),
    mpatches.Patch(facecolor='#da3633', edgecolor='#30363d', label='URGENT (critical)'),
]
leg3 = fig.legend(handles=gap_legend, loc='lower center', bbox_to_anchor=(0.5, legend_y),
                  ncol=4, fontsize=10, facecolor='#161b22', edgecolor='#30363d',
                  labelcolor='white', title='Skill Gap', title_fontsize=11)
leg3.get_title().set_color('#58a6ff')

# ── Summary stats at bottom ─────────────────────────────────────────────
n_urgent = np.sum(gap > 1.5)
n_gap = np.sum((gap > 0.5) & (gap <= 1.5))
n_ok = np.sum((gap >= -0.5) & (gap <= 0.5))
n_strong = np.sum(gap < -0.5)

summary = (f"Summary:  {int(n_urgent)} URGENT gaps  |  {int(n_gap)} moderate gaps  |  "
           f"{int(n_ok)} matched  |  {int(n_strong)} surplus strengths    "
           f"—  Corvia demonstrates {int(np.sum(corvia_level >= 2))}/50 skills at Moderate+ level")
fig.text(0.5, 0.005, summary, fontsize=11, color='#8b949e', ha='center',
         style='italic')

plt.savefig('/root/corvia/docs/ai_jobs_heatmap.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight',
            facecolor='#0d1117', edgecolor='none')
plt.close()
print("Heatmap saved to /root/corvia/docs/ai_jobs_heatmap.png")

# ── Print text summary ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
print("\n" + "="*70)
print("  SKILLS GAP SUMMARY — AI Infra Jobs Malaysia vs Corvia Portfolio")
print("="*70)

print(f"\n🔴 URGENT GAPS ({int(n_urgent)} skills) — High demand, not in Corvia:")
for i in range(n):
    if gap[i] > 1.5:
        print(f"   • {skill_names[i]:35s}  demand={avg_demand[i]:.1f}  you={int(corvia_level[i])}")

print(f"\n🟡 MODERATE GAPS ({int(n_gap)} skills) — Important, partially covered:")
for i in range(n):
    if 0.5 < gap[i] <= 1.5:
        print(f"   • {skill_names[i]:35s}  demand={avg_demand[i]:.1f}  you={int(corvia_level[i])}")

print(f"\n🟢 STRONG POINTS ({int(n_strong)} skills) — You exceed market demand:")
for i in range(n):
    if gap[i] < -0.5:
        print(f"   ★ {skill_names[i]:35s}  demand={avg_demand[i]:.1f}  you={int(corvia_level[i])}")

print(f"\n✅ MATCHED ({int(n_ok)} skills) — Demand ≈ Your level:")
for i in range(n):
    if -0.5 <= gap[i] <= 0.5:
        print(f"   ✓ {skill_names[i]:35s}  demand={avg_demand[i]:.1f}  you={int(corvia_level[i])}")
